name: Ruby
description: An elegant, developer-friendly language famous for Ruby on Rails. Optimized for programmer happiness.

lessons.title: Hello World & puts
lessons.content: Ruby is clean and readable — often reads like English. The 'puts' method prints output followed by a newline. 'print' outputs without a newline. String interpolation uses #{} inside double-quoted strings.
lessons.code_example:
puts "Hello, World!"
puts 42

name = "Alice"
puts "Hello, #{name}!"  # interpolation
puts 1 + 2               # 3

exercises.question: Which Ruby method prints output with a newline?
What syntax is used for string interpolation in Ruby?
hints: "Hello, #{name}!" — hash + curly braces inside double quotes

lessons.title: Arrays & Hashes
lessons.content: Ruby arrays hold ordered collections. Hashes are key-value stores (like dictionaries). Both are very flexible. Key array methods: push, pop, map, select, each. Hashes use => or symbol: syntax.
lessons.code_example:
fruits = ['apple', 'banana', 'cherry']
fruits.push('mango')
puts fruits.length  # 4

doubled = fruits.map { |f| f.upcase }
puts doubled.inspect

person = { name: 'Alice', age: 28 }
puts person[:name]  # Alice

exercises.question: What is a Ruby Hash equivalent to in other languages?

lessons.title: Classes & Methods
lessons.content: Ruby is fully object-oriented — everything is an object. Define classes with 'class'. Use 'def' to define methods. 'initialize' is the constructor. Instance variables start with @.
lessons.code_example:
class Dog
  def initialize(name)
    @name = name
  end

  def speak
    "Woof! I'm #{@name}."
  end
end

dog = Dog.new('Rex')
puts dog.speak  # Woof! I'm Rex.

exercises.question: What prefix do instance variables use in Ruby?

lessons.title: Blocks, Procs & Lambdas
lessons.content: Blocks are anonymous chunks of code passed to methods using do..end or {}. Procs and Lambdas are reusable blocks stored as objects. They are central to Ruby's expressive style.
lessons.code_example:
# Block with each
[1, 2, 3].each do |n|
  puts n * 2
end

# Lambda
square = ->(x) { x * x }
puts square.call(5)  # 25

# Proc
double = Proc.new { |x| x * 2 }
puts double.call(4)  # 8

exercises.question: Which syntax creates a Lambda in Ruby?
